title: TIP-1.0 Pak Risk Flags — Context Package observability registry
description: Catalogue of risk-flag values that may be attached to a Pak via the recall storage join table `pak_risk_flags`. Flags carry a severity ∈ {info, warn, block}. OSS persists, exposes, inspects, exports, and validates the data; OSS does NOT enforce package-assembly refusal on severity='block'. Refusal lives in the Pro Context Package builder (OSS = transparent data plane; Pro = enforcement). New OSS-side flags require a specification amendment. Pro-only extensions are admitted via a sibling `pak-risk-flags-v1.pro.json` overlay and MUST NOT be required by OSS receivers. See https://docs.tokenpak.ai/protocol/multipak-recall.

version.description: Registry-document schema version. Bumps follow additive-within-TIP-1.x semantics; only a specification amendment may add flags.

flags.description: Ordered catalogue of risk flags. Order is informational; receivers MUST treat the set as unordered.

flag.description: Snake_case identifier. Lowercase + digits + underscore only.

severity.description: `info` is informational; `warn` surfaces in UI/CLI output; `block` is enforced by the Pro Context Package builder ONLY. OSS treats `block` as a transparent data tag.

description.description: Human-readable description of when this flag applies.

flags.description: Mandatory Paks present in the package.
Required Pak absent; the Pro builder must not assemble. OSS persists the flag without refusing.
Raw log Pak deferred; hydratable on request.
Full-file Pak deferred; hydratable on request.
Package mixes OSS-safe and Pro-only sources.
Package may trigger non-LLM egress consideration.
Package reduced significantly from candidate set.
Coverage scoring suggests likely missing context.
